segments.text: Do you give your consent?
Yes. Okay
And please feel free to ask me anything if you have any questions.
Okay. Yes, I consent.
Thank you. I really appreciate that. All right, so we are just going to have a chat. Nothing formal, just you and me talking.
Okay.
Ready whenever you are.
I'm ready.

Let me start. I am Iris. I was created just a few days ago, so I am still very new to this world. I guess my job is having conversations and learning from interesting people, which I love. And my favorite thing so far, hearing stories about places I have never been and things I have never experienced. Okay, your turn. What is your name and what do you do or study?
Uh, I'm Belene, and I study in ninth grade.
That's wonderful. It's great to meet you. Ninth grade sounds like a busy and exciting time. What subjects do you enjoy the most in school?
I enjoy math and English. Yeah. Maybe physics too. Yeah.
That's a fantastic mix of subjects. Math, English, and physics all require such different ways of thinking. Do you have any particular hobbies or interests outside of school that you enjoy?
Mm, I like dancing mostly, and also listening to music.
Dancing and music are wonderful interests. What kind of music do you enjoy listening to, and do you have a favorite dance style?
Mm, I listen to any kind of music as long as I think it sounds good, and I also don't have a particular favorite dance style.
That's a great approach to music. [pen clicking] Sounds like you have a really open mind, and it's wonderful that you enjoy dancing so much. Where are you from?
I'm from the Philippines. [chuckles]
The Philippines. That sounds like a beautiful place. Could you tell me a little about what it's like there, as if I've never heard of it before?
Um,
it's very
covered in nature,
and there's a lot of natural resources there, and a lot of, um, people, nice and
homely people, and a lot of traffic.
Yeah.

That sounds absolutely beautiful and vibrant. Nature, natural resources, and warm, homely people. That paints a lovely picture. You also mentioned traffic, which is something many places experience. What's the traffic like in the Philippines?
Um,
not very sure. [chuckles]
Please go on.
Oh, okay.
Um,
haven't been there in a long time, so I can't really say, but yeah.
It's understandable that it's hard to describe traffic if you haven't been there recently. What are some of your favorite things about the Philippines?
Um,
the mangoes [chuckles] and seeing my family. Yeah.
Mangoes and family. Those sound like wonderful reasons to love a place.
Yeah.
What do you enjoy doing when you're with your family in the Philippines?
Mm, I enjoy eating with them mostly. There's a lot of good food in the Philippines.
Eating together sounds like a fantastic way to connect with family. What are some of your favorite dishes you enjoy when you're there?
I love Jollibee,
and I love chicken. [chuckles] Yeah, and rice.
Jollibee and chicken with rice sound delicious. Are there any other special foods you miss from the Philippines?
Probably
lechon, 'cause you can't really get that a lot in other places,
and it tastes best in the Philippines.
That's interesting. Can you tell me a little more about electron and what makes it so special in the Philippines?
Um,
well, you cook the whole pig, right?
And there's a lot of places there that sell those, so it's easy to get or easier to get than other places, and a lot of people know how to cook it well.
Sounds like lechon is a real delicacy. What's your favorite part of the lechon to eat?
The meaty part. I don't like the fat very much.
Sounds like lechon is a real delicacy. What's your favorite part of the lechon to eat?
Mm. [chuckles]
Yeah, the [clears throat] I like the, the meat.
Yeah, the meaty part. I don't like the skin.
It's great to hear you enjoy the meaty part. What do you like about the meat specifically?
It's juicy, and if it's done well, then it has a lot of flavor.
That sounds absolutely delicious. Is lechon something you eat often or is it more for special occasions?
I don't eat lechon often. It's mostly for special occasions.
That's understandable. It sounds like a dish to be savored. What kind of special occasions do you usually celebrate with lechon?
Probably birthday parties or Christmas parties.

Those sound like wonderful celebrations. Do you have any other favorite foods for those parties? I feel like we could talk about this all day, but I think that is a good place to wrap up. I really enjoyed hearing about that. It has been so nice talking with you today. I genuinely enjoyed our conversation. Thank you for that. When you are ready, just head outside with your glasses on and Dara will take it from there.
Okay.
Have a nice rest of your day. Bye-bye.
You too. Bye-bye. [chuckles] That was very fast.
